heaviest_rainfall.status: The largest amount of rain ever recorded is on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm fell at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour over the 144.8 hour event.

heaviest_rainrate.status: 34.3mm per hour is the maximum rain rate ever recorded on 02 September 2025, during a 15.0 hour storm which dumped 21.19mm of rain from the clouds.

longest_rain.status: The longest rain storm on record lasted 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The lightning strike count peaked at 23 on 07 September 2025, the highest since recording started on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: No stormy weather is anticipated.  The last big storm was recorded on 02 January 2026, lasting 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: Right now, a cold snap is in effect, it started on 2026-01-01 and has continued for {days} day{plurals}.  There has been 1 cold snap this year; usually, 3.6 occurs annually.

extreme.status: The largest temperature gap recorded is 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C) on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no heatwaves in progress.  So far, 0 heatwavess have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 18 July 2025.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 62.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (196°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The largest wind gust ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The largest wind speed ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 4.2°C), outside temperature (down 4.1°C), and inside temperature (down 2.8°C).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 111 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in inside temperature (up 6.2°C) and cloud levels (up 113 m) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 111.4m lower than yesterday, 113.5m lower than the past week, 104.2m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 845m to a maximum of 1717m, averaging 1087.3m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 1200.8m and 1191.5m respectively.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures will drop to chilly levels. Outside; bitterly cold temperatures are expected. Also, calm and still conditions are expected. A few light raindrops may fall, occasional overcast conditions, and dim or diffuse light conditions. Little change in the weather.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (0.6°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
